title: SEC Test Baseline Checklist
content: This SEC test artifact provides a practical baseline for validating a secure backend pipeline. Scope: verify input validation, authentication boundaries, authorization checks, secret handling, logging hygiene, and error response consistency. Input validation: enforce strict schema validation, reject unknown fields, sanitize untrusted strings, and block oversized payloads with clear 4xx responses. Authentication: require signed tokens, validate issuer/audience/expiration, reject clock-skew abuse, and rotate signing keys with overlap windows. Authorization: apply least privilege per endpoint, test horizontal and vertical access control, and ensure denied requests never leak resource existence. Data protection: encrypt data in transit with modern TLS, encrypt sensitive fields at rest, and avoid storing raw credentials or tokens. Secrets: load from managed secret storage, scope by environment, rotate regularly, and prevent exposure in logs, traces, and crash dumps. Logging and monitoring: create structured logs with request IDs, redact PII and secrets, emit security events for failed auth and policy violations, and alert on anomaly thresholds. Error handling: return generic error messages externally, preserve detailed diagnostics internally, and keep response timing consistent to reduce side-channel clues. Dependency and supply-chain controls: pin versions, run vulnerability scans in CI, verify package integrity, and gate deployments on severity policy. Operational checks: enforce rate limits, add IP/device heuristics where relevant, protect admin routes with stronger controls, and validate backup/restore integrity. Test execution notes: run automated regression tests per commit, perform periodic manual abuse-case testing, document findings with severity and remediation owner, and track closure SLA. Expected output quality: reproducible test evidence, minimal false positives, and actionable remediation items aligned to service risk profile.
